records.chapter: Chapter on the Types of Marriage
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4339 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, from al-Husayn ibn Zayd, who said that he heard Abu Abdillah (as) say: "Marital relations are made lawful through three means-marriage with inheritance, marriage without inheritance, and marriage through ownership by the right hand."
records.arabicText: 4339 - رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: "تَحِلُّ اَلْفُرُوجُ بِثَلاَثَةِ وُجُوهٍ نِكَاحٍ بِمِيرَاثٍ وَ نِكَاحٍ بِلاَ مِيرَاثٍ وَ نِكَاحٍ بِمِلْكِ اَلْيَمِينِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4339 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Ziyad, from al-Husayn ibn Zayd, who said that he heard Abu Abdillah (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Marital relations are made lawful through three means-marriage with inheritance, marriage without inheritance, and marriage through ownership by the right hand."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Marriage
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4340 - It is narrated from Amr ibn Shimr, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Ali al-Baqir (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"What prevents a believer from taking a spouse? Perhaps Allah (swt) will grant him offspring who will fill the earth with 'There is no god but Allah (swt).'"
records.arabicText: 4340 - رُوِيَ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ اَلْبَاقِرِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَا يَمْنَعُ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ أَهْلاً لَعَلَّ اَللَّهُ أَنْ يَرْزُقَهُ نَسَمَةً تُثْقِلُ اَلْأَرْضَ بِلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4340 - It is narrated from Amr ibn Shimr, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Ali al-Baqir (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "What prevents a believer from taking a spouse? Perhaps Allah (swt) will grant him offspring who will fill the earth with 'There is no god but Allah (swt).'"

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Marriage
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4341 - It is narrated from Mu'ammar ibn Khallad that Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said: "Three practices are from the traditions of the messengers, using perfume, removing excess hair, and frequently visiting one's wife (or wives)."
records.arabicText: 4341 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَمَّرِ بْنِ خَلاَّدٍ عَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: "ثَلاَثٌ مِنْ سُنَنِ اَلْمُرْسَلِينَ اَلْعِطْرُ وَ إِحْفَاءُ اَلشَّعْرِ وَ كَثْرَةُ اَلطَّرُوقَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4341 - It is narrated from Mu'ammar ibn Khallad that Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Three practices are from the traditions of the messengers, using perfume, removing excess hair, and frequently visiting one's wife (or wives)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Marriage
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4342 - It is narrated from al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Abdillah (as), that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Whoever gets married has safeguarded half of his religion."
In another narration, it is added: "So let him fear Allah (swt) regarding the remaining half."
records.arabicText:
4342 - وَ قَدْ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ تَزَوَّجَ أَحْرَزَ نِصْفَ دِينِهِ".
وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ : "فَلْيَتَّقِ اَللَّهَ فِي اَلنِّصْفِ اَلْبَاقِي".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4342 - It is narrated from al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Abdillah (as), that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever gets married has safeguarded half of his religion."
In another narration, it is added: "So let him fear Allah (swt) regarding the remaining half."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Marriage
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4343 - Abdullah ibn al-Hakam narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"No structure in Islam is more beloved to Allah (swt), the Exalted, than marriage."
records.arabicText: 4343 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَا بُنِيَ بِنَاءٌ فِي اَلْإِسْلاَمِ أَحَبُّ إِلَى اَللَّهِ تَعَالَى مِنَ اَلتَّزْوِيجِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4343 - Abdullah ibn al-Hakam narrated from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No structure in Islam is more beloved to Allah (swt), the Exalted, than marriage."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Marriage
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4344 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim that Abu Abdillah (as) said that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Marry, for I will take pride in your numbers before the nations on the Day of Judgment. Even the miscarried child will stand at the gate of Paradise, refusing to enter until his parents enter Paradise before him."
records.arabicText: 4344 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ أَنَّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ: "تَزَوَّجُوا فَإِنِّي مُكَاثِرٌ بِكُمُ اَلْأُمَمَ غَداً فِي اَلْقِيَامَةِ حَتَّى إِنَّ اَلسِّقْطَ لَيَجِيءُ مُحْبَنْطِئاً عَلَى بَابِ اَلْجَنَّةِ فَيُقَالُ لَهُ اُدْخُلِ اَلْجَنَّةَ فَيَقُولُ لاَ حَتَّى يَدْخُلَ أَبَوَايَ اَلْجَنَّةَ قَبْلِي".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4344 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim that Abu Abdillah (as) said that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Marry, for I will take pride in your numbers before the nations on the Day of Judgment. Even the miscarried child will stand at the gate of Paradise, refusing to enter until his parents enter Paradise before him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Virtue of Marriage
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4345 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Take a spouse, for it is a means of increasing your sustenance."
records.arabicText: 4345 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "اِتَّخَذُوا اَلْأَهْلَ فَإِنَّهُ أَرْزَقُ لَكُمْ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4345 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Take a spouse, for it is a means of increasing your sustenance."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Superiority of the Married Person Over the Unmarried Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4346 - Abdullah ibn Maymun narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) (peace be upon them), who said:
"Two units of prayer performed by a married person are better than seventy units of prayer performed by an unmarried person."
records.arabicText: 4346 - رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ مَيْمُونٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "رَكْعَتَانِ يُصَلِّيهِمَا مُتَزَوِّجٌ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ رَكْعَةً يُصَلِّيهِمَا أَعْزَبُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4346 - Abdullah ibn Maymun narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) (peace be upon them), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Two units of prayer performed by a married person are better than seventy units of prayer performed by an unmarried person."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Superiority of the Married Person Over the Unmarried Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4347 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Two units of prayer performed by a married person are better than an unmarried man who spends his nights in prayer and his days fasting."
records.arabicText: 4347 - قَالَ وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "لَرَكْعَتَانِ يُصَلِّيهِمَا مُتَزَوِّجٌ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ رَجُلٍ عَزَبٍ يَقُومُ لَيْلَهُ وَ يَصُومُ نَهَارَهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4347 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Two units of prayer performed by a married person are better than an unmarried man who spends his nights in prayer and his days fasting."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Superiority of the Married Person Over the Unmarried Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4348 - It is narrated that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said: "Indeed, the most despicable of your dead are the unmarried."
records.arabicText: 4348 - وَ رُوِيَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ: "إِنَّ أَرَاذِلَ مَوْتَاكُمُ اَلْعُزَّابُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4348 - It is narrated that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, the most despicable of your dead are the unmarried."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Superiority of the Married Person Over the Unmarried Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4349 - It has been narrated that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "The majority of the inhabitants of the Fire are the unmarried."
records.arabicText: 4349 - وَ رُوِيَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ: "أَكْثَرُ أَهْلِ اَلنَّارِ اَلْعُزَّابُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4349 - It has been narrated that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The majority of the inhabitants of the Fire are the unmarried."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Affection for Women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4350 - Abu Malik al-Hadrami narrated from Abu al-Abbas, who said that he heard Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say: "The more love a servant has for women, the greater he increases in faith and virtue."
records.arabicText: 4350 - رَوَى أَبُو مَالِكٍ اَلْحَضْرَمِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْعَبَّاسِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: "اَلْعَبْدُ كُلَّمَا اِزْدَادَ لِلنِّسَاءِ حُبّاً اِزْدَادَ فِي اَلْإِيمَانِ فَضْلاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4350 - Abu Malik al-Hadrami narrated from Abu al-Abbas, who said that he heard Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The more love a servant has for women, the greater he increases in faith and virtue."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Affection for Women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4351 - In a narration from Aban, from Umar ibn Yazid, Abu Abdillah (as) said: "I do not think that a man increases in faith and goodness except that he also increases in love for women."
records.arabicText: 4351 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ أَبَانٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "مَا أَظُنُّ رَجُلاً يَزْدَادُ فِي اَلْإِيمَانِ خَيْراً إِلاَّ اِزْدَادَ حُبّاً لِلنِّسَاءِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4351 - In a narration from Aban, from Umar ibn Yazid, Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I do not think that a man increases in faith and goodness except that he also increases in love for women."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Abundance of Goodness in Women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4352 - It is narrated from Ibn Faddal, from Yunus ibn Ya'qub, from someone who heard Abu Abdillah (as) say: "Most of the good is found in women."
records.arabicText: 4352 - رُوِيَ عَنِ اِبْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ يَعْقُوبَ عَمَّنْ سَمِعَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: "أَكْثَرُ اَلْخَيْرِ فِي اَلنِّسَاءِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4352 - It is narrated from Ibn Faddal, from Yunus ibn Ya'qub, from someone who heard Abu Abdillah (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Most of the good is found in women."

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Avoids Marriage Due to Fear of Poverty
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4353 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr, from Hariz, from al-Walid, who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said: "Whoever avoids marriage out of fear of poverty has thought poorly of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says: 'If they are poor, Allah (swt) will enrich them out of His bounty' (Surah An-Nur 24:32)."
records.arabicText:
4353 - رُوِيَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ عَنِ اَلْوَلِيدِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ تَرَكَ اَلتَّزْوِيجَ مَخَافَةَ اَلْفَقْرِ فَقَدْ أَسَاءَ اَلظَّنَّ بِاللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ:
۝ إِنْ يَكُونُوا فُقَرٰاءَ يُغْنِهِمُ اَللّٰهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ۝ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4353 - It is narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr, from Hariz, from al-Walid, who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever avoids marriage out of fear of poverty has thought poorly of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says: 'If they are poor, Allah (swt) will enrich them out of His bounty' (Surah An-Nur 24:32)."

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Avoids Marriage Due to Fear of Poverty
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4354 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said: "Whoever desires to meet Allah (swt) in a state of purity and purification, let him meet Him with a spouse. And whoever avoids marriage out of fear of poverty has thought poorly of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious."
records.arabicText: 4354 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ سَرَّهُ أَنْ يَلْقَى اَللَّهَ طَاهِراً مُطَهَّراً فَلْيَلْقَهُ بِزَوْجَةٍ وَ مَنْ تَرَكَ اَلتَّزْوِيجَ مَخَافَةَ اَلْعَيْلَةِ فَقَدْ أَسَاءَ اَلظَّنَّ بِاللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4354 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever desires to meet Allah (swt) in a state of purity and purification, let him meet Him with a spouse. And whoever avoids marriage out of fear of poverty has thought poorly of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious."

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Marries for the Sake of Allah {swt}, the Almighty, and to Uphold Family Ties
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4355 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as), Sayyid al-'Abideen (as), said:
"Whoever marries for the sake of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, and to maintain family ties, Allah (swt), the Exalted, will crown him with the crown of sovereignty and honor."
[cha[ter=CHAPTER 104 - CHAPTER ON THE BEST OF WOMEN]
بَابُ أَفْضَلِ النِّسَاءِ
records.arabicText: 4355 - قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ سَيِّدُ اَلْعَابِدِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ تَزَوَّجَ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ لِصِلَةِ اَلرَّحِمِ تَوَّجَهُ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى بِتَاجِ اَلْمُلْكِ وَ اَلْكَرَامَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4355 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as), Sayyid al-'Abideen (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever marries for the sake of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, and to maintain family ties, Allah (swt), the Exalted, will crown him with the crown of sovereignty and honor."
[cha[ter=CHAPTER 104 - CHAPTER ON THE BEST OF WOMEN]
بَابُ أَفْضَلِ النِّسَاءِ

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Marries for the Sake of Allah {swt}, the Almighty, and to Uphold Family Ties
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4356 - Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated from al-Sadiq Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers (peace be upon them) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"The best women of my nation are those with the most radiant faces and the lowest dowries."
records.arabicText: 4356 - رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "أَفْضَلُ نِسَاءِ أُمَّتِي أَصْبَحُهُنَّ وَجْهاً وَ أَقَلُّهُنَّ مَهْراً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4356 - Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated from al-Sadiq Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), from his forefathers (peace be upon them) that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The best women of my nation are those with the most radiant faces and the lowest dowries."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Categories of Women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4357 - Mas'adah ibn Ziyad narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) (peace be upon them) that he said:
"Women are of four types, there are those who are like a fertile spring, those who are abundant and productive, those who are burdensome and oppressive, and those who are like a shackle infested with lice."
records.arabicText: 4357 - رُوِيَ عَنْ مَسْعَدَةَ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "اَلنِّسَاءُ أَرْبَعَةُ أَصْنَافٍ فَمِنْهُنَّ رَبِيعٌ مُرْبِعٌ وَ مِنْهُنَّ جَامِعٌ مُجْمِعٌ وَ مِنْهُنَّ كَرْبٌ مُقْمِعٌ وَ مِنْهُنَّ غُلٌّ قَمِلٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4357 - Mas'adah ibn Ziyad narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) (peace be upon them) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Women are of four types, there are those who are like a fertile spring, those who are abundant and productive, those who are burdensome and oppressive, and those who are like a shackle infested with lice."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Categories of Women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4358 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Dawud al-Karkhi, who said: I said to Abu Abdillah (as), "My wife has passed away. She was compatible with me, and I am thinking about getting married."
Imam (as) said: "Consider carefully where you place yourself, with whom you share your wealth, and to whom you reveal your religion, secrets, and trust. If you must proceed, then marry a virgin who is known for goodness and good character.
Know that women are created differently: among them are those who are a blessing and those who are a burden; among them are like the crescent moon that delights its owner when it appears; and among them are like darkness. Whoever finds a righteous woman is happy, and whoever is deceived by one has no recourse for revenge.
Women are of three types:
A woman who is fertile, loving, and supports her husband in his worldly and religious affairs, not helping life's hardships against him.
A woman who is barren, neither beautiful nor of good character, and does not help her husband in anything good.
And a woman who is loud, argumentative, fault-finding, belittles much, and is not content with little."
records.arabicText:
4358 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ اَلْكَرْخِيِّ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ صَاحِبَتِي هَلَكَتْ وَ كَانَتْ لِي مُوَافِقَةً وَ قَدْ هَمَمْتُ أَنْ أَتَزَوَّجَ فَقَالَ "اُنْظُرْ أَيْنَ تَضَعُ نَفْسَكَ وَ مَنْ تُشْرِكُهُ فِي مَالِكَ وَ تُطْلِعُهُ عَلَى دِينِكَ وَ سِرِّكَ وَ أَمَانَتِكَ فَإِنْ كُنْتَ لاَ بُدَّ فَاعِلاً فَبِكْراً تُنْسَبُ إِلَى اَلْخَيْرِ وَ إِلَى حُسْنِ اَلْخُلُقِ،
أَلاَ إِنَّ اَلنِّسَاءَ خُلِقْنَ شَتَّى فَمِنْهُنَّ اَلْغَنِيمَةُ وَ اَلْغَرَامُ وَ مِنْهُنَّ اَلْهِلاَلُ إِذَا تَجَلَّى لِصَاحِبِهِ وَ مِنْهُنَّ اَلظَّلاَمُ فَمَنْ يَظْفَرْ بِصَالِحِهِنَّ يَسْعَدْ وَ مَنْ يُغْبَنْ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ اِنْتِقَامُ وَ هُنَّ ثَلاَثٌ فَامْرَأَةٌ وَلُودٌ وَدُودٌ، تُعِينُ زَوْجَهَا عَلَى دَهْرِهِ لِدُنْيَاهُ وَ آخِرَتِهِ وَ لاَ تُعِينُ اَلدَّهْرَ عَلَيْهِ وَ اِمْرَأَةٌ عَقِيمٌ لاَ ذَاتُ جَمَالٍ وَ لاَ خُلُقٍ وَ لاَ تُعِينُ زَوْجَهَا عَلَى خَيْرٍ وَ اِمْرَأَةٌ صَخَّابَةٌ وَلاَّجَةٌ هَمَّازَةٌ تَسْتَقِلُّ اَلْكَثِيرَ وَ لاَ تَقْبَلُ اَلْيَسِيرَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4358 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Dawud al-Karkhi, who said: I said to Abu Abdillah (as),
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"My wife has passed away. She was compatible with me, and I am thinking about getting married."
Imam (as) said: "Consider carefully where you place yourself, with whom you share your wealth, and to whom you reveal your religion, secrets, and trust. If you must proceed, then marry a virgin who is known for goodness and good character.
Know that women are created differently: among them are those who are a blessing and those who are a burden; among them are like the crescent moon that delights its owner when it appears; and among them are like darkness. Whoever finds a righteous woman is happy, and whoever is deceived by one has no recourse for revenge.
Women are of three types:
A woman who is fertile, loving, and supports her husband in his worldly and religious affairs, not helping life's hardships against him.
A woman who is barren, neither beautiful nor of good character, and does not help her husband in anything good.
And a woman who is loud, argumentative, fault-finding, belittles much, and is not content with little."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blessing and Omen of a Woman
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4359 - It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Bukayr, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
"Among the blessings of a woman are the lightness of her expenses and the ease of her childbirth. And among her misfortunes are the heaviness of her expenses and the difficulty of her childbirth."
records.arabicText: 4359 - رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مِنْ بَرَكَةِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ خِفَّةُ مَئُونَتِهَا وَ تَيْسِيرُ وِلاَدَتِهَا وَ مِنْ شُؤْمِهَا شِدَّةُ مَئُونَتِهَا وَ تَعْسِيرُ وِلاَدَتِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4359 - It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Bukayr, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Among the blessings of a woman are the lightness of her expenses and the ease of her childbirth. And among her misfortunes are the heaviness of her expenses and the difficulty of her childbirth."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blessing and Omen of a Woman
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4360 - It is narrated: "Among the blessings of a woman is the smallness of her dowry, and among her misfortunes is the largeness of her dowry."
records.arabicText: 4360 - وَ رُوِيَ: "أَنَّ مِنْ بَرَكَةِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ قِلَّةَ مَهْرِهَا وَ مِنْ شُؤْمِهَا كَثْرَةَ مَهْرِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4360 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Among the blessings of a woman is the smallness of her dowry, and among her misfortunes is the largeness of her dowry."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blessing and Omen of a Woman
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4361 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said: "Marry women with blue eyes, for indeed there is blessing in them."
records.arabicText: 4361 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "تَزَوَّجُوا اَلزُّرْقَ فَإِنَّ فِيهِنَّ اَلْبَرَكَةَ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Recommended and Praiseworthy Traits and Characteristics of Women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4362 - Commander of the Faithful (as) said: "Marry a woman who is dark-skinned, wide-eyed, full-figured, and of medium height. If you dislike her, then I will bear the dowry."
records.arabicText: 4362 - قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "تَزَوَّجْ سَمْرَاءَ عَيْنَاءَ عَجْزَاءَ مَرْبُوعَةً فَإِنْ كَرِهْتَهَا فَعَلَيَّ اَلصَّدَاقُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4362 - Commander of the Faithful (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Marry a woman who is dark-skinned, wide-eyed, full-figured, and of medium height. If you dislike her, then I will bear the dowry."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Recommended and Praiseworthy Traits and Characteristics of Women
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4363 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), when intending to marry a woman, would send someone to look at her and said: "Smell her neck, for if her neck smells good, her scent will also be pleasant. And if her ankles are fleshy, her private parts will be well-developed."
records.arabicText: 4363 - وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ اِمْرَأَةً بَعَثَ إِلَيْهَا مَنْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهَا وَ قَالَ "شَمِّي لِيتَهَا فَإِنْ طَابَ لِيتُهَا طَابَ عَرْفُهَا وَ إِنْ دَرِمَ كَعْبُهَا عَظُمَ كَعْثَبُهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4363 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family), when intending to marry a woman, would send someone to look at her and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Smell her neck, for if her neck smells good, her scent will also be pleasant. And if her ankles are fleshy, her private parts will be well-developed."
